message:
perf(storage): skip per-insert upsert of bootstrap LOCAL source row

Every conversation insert called ensure_source_for_conversation(),
which upserted the local-source row even when the conversation was
just normalized to the canonical bootstrap LOCAL_SOURCE_ID with no
host/machine/platform metadata attached. open() and the schema-repair
paths already seed that exact row, so the per-insert upsert was a
hot-path autocommit UPDATE that produced no change.

Add is_bootstrap_local_source(): true iff the source matches the
bootstrap shape — id == LOCAL_SOURCE_ID, kind == Local, and every
metadata field (host_label, machine_id, platform, config_json,
created_at, updated_at) is None. ensure_source_for_conversation()
and the bulk insert_conversations dedup loop now early-return for
that shape and skip upsert_source(). Non-bootstrap sources, including
remote sources and any local source that has metadata attached, still
go through upsert_source() unchanged.

Add a regression test
repeated_local_inserts_do_not_touch_bootstrap_source_row that:
- captures sources.updated_at after open()
- inserts two LOCAL conversations through insert_conversation_tree()
- asserts the bootstrap row's updated_at is byte-for-byte unchanged
  after both inserts (millisecond sleeps between calls would otherwise
  observe a bumped updated_at if the upsert still ran).

This is a pure optimization: the visible state of the sources table is
identical before and after the change for the bootstrap row.
